classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

source.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: Magnificently and divinely, therefore, our God said to his servant: "I am that I am," and "Thou shalt say to the children of Israel, He who is sent me to you."

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: For He truly is because He is unchangeable.

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: For every change makes what was not, to be: therefore He truly is, who is unchangeable; but all other things that were made by Him have received being from Him each in its own measure.

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: To Him who is highest, therefore nothing can be contrary, save what is not; and consequently as from Him everything that is good has its being, so from Him is everything that by nature exists; since everything that exists by nature is good.

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: Thus every nature is good, and everything good is from God; therefore every nature is from God.
